title: sambucus racemosa subsp. pendula
intro: Sambucus racemosa subsp. pendula, also known as the weeping elderberry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its arching branches and clusters of small, fragrant white flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, boasting medicinal properties such as antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds, particularly in its berries and flowers. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa subsp. pendula include its ability to boost the immune system, alleviate cold and flu symptoms, and provide relief from allergies and sinus congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and capsules made from its berries and flowers, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
